items.title: A practical guide to targeted single-cell RNA sequencing technologies
items.author: Konrad Basler
items.content: A review of targeted single-cell RNA sequencing methods provides a practical guide to help researchers select the method most suited for their own experiments.

items.title: Mitochondrial energetic failure underlies FLVCR1-related sensory neuropathy
items.author: Deborah Chiabrando
items.content: Functional studies of pathogenetic FLVCR1 variants associated with sensory neuropathy reveal that impaired choline uptake, heme biosynthesis, and ER–mitochondria Ca²⁺ transfer result in mitochondrial energetic failure.

items.title: Ultrafast and specific miRNA quantification via single-molecule fluorescence quenching kinetics
items.author: Sungchul Hohng
items.content: Single-molecule fluorescence quenching dynamics enable more than a 600-fold increase in miRNA detection speed, allowing ultra-fast and highly specific identification of multiple miRNAs within seconds.

items.title: Transcriptomic and metabolomic insights into gabapentin’s therapeutic role in neurogenic inflammation of rosacea
items.author: Bin Wei
items.content: Gabapentin alleviates rosacea by suppressing neurogenic inflammation via NF-κB pathway, improving erythema and inflammation in LL37-induced models and patients, revealed by integrated transcriptomic and metabolomic analyses.

items.title: A two-step actin-mediated strategy enables Campylobacter jejuni to promote mitochondrial aggregation and iron homeostasis, for intracellular survival and persistence
items.author: Brendan W. Wren
items.content: Campylobacter jejuni deploys secreted invasion antigens to sequentially remodel host actin, redirecting mitochondria and iron handling in Acanthamoeba castellanii to stabilise an intracellular niche that supports long-term persistence.

items.title: Exploring microbial diversity using cell-size fractionated enrichment incubations from subsurface aquifers at Äspö, Sweden
items.author: Mark Dopson
items.content: Enrichment incubations of subsurface groundwaters (Äspö, Sweden) reveal a highly diverse microbial community dominated by small cells, with strong co-occurrences among populations that may support survival in this low-energy environment.

items.title: A dual respiratory and auditory function for the coelacanth lung
items.author: Lionel Cavin
items.content: Synchrotron imaging of fossil and extant coelacanths reveals that the lung of extinct species likely served both respiratory and auditory functions, transmitting sound pressure to the inner ear via a specialized perilymphatic system

items.title: Tau is necessary for Pseudomonas aeruginosa-induced blood-brain barrier dysfunction
items.author: Amy R. Nelson
items.content: Pre-clinical models of bacterial pneumonia reveal an important role of tau in systemic infections. Pneumonia causes AD-like tau phosphorylation in the brain. Additionally, tau is required for pneumonia-induced neurovascular dysfunction.
